subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's central domain is the properties of magnets — natural (lodestones) and artificial — including poles (North and South always in pairs), the directional property of a freely suspended magnet, Earth as a giant magnet, and the attraction and repulsion rules between unlike and like poles. Every section and activity in the chapter is an inquiry into some aspect of magnet behaviour, precisely matching C-2.3's mandate to describe the properties of a magnet (natural and artificial; Earth as a magnet).

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to describe and investigate the properties of magnets by observing how magnets attract certain materials, how magnetic force concentrates at poles, how a freely suspended magnet aligns north-south due to Earth's magnetic nature, and how like and unlike poles interact.
